title: 11 Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit
body_text:
11	Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit
11.1	Overview of Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit
There are four Branch Capture user types: Capture Users, Reviewers, Supervisors, and Administrators. Based on how you assign user types, a userГўВҖВҷs tasks, experiences, and responsibilities vary. See Section 8.2, Branch Capture Workflows and User Types, for more information. The procedures in this chapter apply to Supervisor Users and/or Supervisor Users who are also assigned other Branch Capture user types (e.g., Capture User and/or Reviewer User).
Supervisor Users are responsible for reviewing and transmitting a Capture UserГўВҖВҷs deposits to Central 1 for processing.
Note:	Transmitting deposits doesnГўВҖВҷt apply to Capture users who are also assigned the Reviewer and Supervisor user types. For those users, deposits transmit to Central 1 when the user selects ГўВҖВңCompleteГўВҖВқ. See Section 8.2, Branch Capture Workflows and User Types, for more information.
11.2	Transmitting a Branch Capture Deposit
Transmit a Branch Capture deposit as follows:
Log in to Branch Capture as described in Section 9.3, Logging in to Branch Capture.
Search for and open the appropriate deposit as described in Section 9.6, Searching for and Viewing a Branch Capture Deposit.
Select the List of Deposits drop-down menu to choose the appropriate date range (if required). The default is ГўВҖВңTodayГўВҖВқ. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Select the Deposit Timeframe
Tip:	Use the State column filter to only display Open-Approved deposits. Select the filter, enter ГўВҖВңApprovedГўВҖВқ in the State Contains field, then select
Apply
. See Figure 2 and Figure 3. Select the ГўВҖВңXГўВҖВқ on the State column to clear the filter. Alternatively, select the State column header to sort deposits by state.
Figure 2: Select the State Column Filter
Figure 3: Filtering the Deposit List to Display ГўВҖВңBalancedГўВҖВқ Deposits
Review the deposit. See Section 9.14, Reviewing a Branch Capture Deposit, for more information.
Important
:	Capture Users can edit or delete a deposit provided that the deposit state isnГўВҖВҷt Open-Approved. Deposits in an Open-Approved state can only be transmitted for processing.
Select
Transmit
, then select
OK
to confirm. See Figure 4. A message displays, confirming the deposit is successfully transmitted to Central 1 for processing. See Figure 5.
Figure 4: Select ГўВҖВңTransmitГўВҖВқ
Figure 5: Deposit Transmission Confirmation
Credit unions in BC and Ontario, and other Central 1 clients, use the ADC and ADUS reports to reconcile deposits to your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs Central 1 accounts as described in Section 17.1, Settlement and Reconciliation of Deposits to your Financial InstitutionГўВҖВҷs Central 1 Account. If Central 1 identifies items that shouldnГўВҖВҷt be cleared (e.g., piggyback items), or items deposited as the wrong deposit type (e.g., a USD item included in a CAD deposit) you will receive a deposit adjustment. Adjustments are reported on the ADC and ADUS reports, and you can view the deposit adjustments and related items in ORS. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part.
Note:	Deposit adjustments are subject to fees. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725. All other clients, contact your relationship manager.
Once a deposit is transmitted for processing, ensure that you store deposit items processed in Branch Capture in a secure location until they are destroyed per your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs retention and destruction policy. See Section 4.9, Policy on Retention and Destruction of Items Processed via Remote Deposit Capture, for more information.
Tip:	Consider printing a Branch Capture Deposit List report to bundle deposits for storage. Wrap the bundle with the Summary section displaying on top to help identify a business dayГўВҖВҷs deposits and retrieve original items for destruction (or rescanning if applicable). See Section 13.3, Generating a Branch Capture Deposit List Report, for procedures.
END
